# UC-22.2.32 · NIS2 Proportional Security Measure Verification by Tier

## Description

Cross-checks the NIS2 control matrix against your active Enterprise Security correlation searches so you can prove required detective controls stay enabled for each entity tier—or expose intentional gaps with a ticket trail.

## Value

Helps proportionality reviews by translating "which tier needs which control" into live Splunk configuration state instead of slide-deck assertions, so internal audit can trace a disabled search back to a named `control_id`.

## Implementation

(1) Prefix every mandated correlation search with `nis2_` + `control_id`. (2) Version `nis2_control_matrix.csv` whenever risk committee moves a control between tiers. (3) Save `UC-22.2.32` weekly. (4) Auto-assign SNOW tasks using Splunkbase 1928 alert action. (5) Mirror outputs into GRC workbook quarterly.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) ≥7.0 deployed to Search Head Cluster; verify app context `SplunkEnterpriseSecurity` exists.
• Service account `svc_es_readout` granted `list_saved_searches` via **Settings > Authentication > Roles** without interactive login.
• CSV `nis2_control_matrix.csv` and `nis2_entity_classification.csv` on Deployment Server with SHA manifest.
• Network ACL allowing REST `8089/tcp` loopback from SH to itself (or SHC captain).
• Understanding of naming convention `nis2_<control_id>` matching matrix rows.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Import matrix from GRC tool. Ensure `required_for_tier` uses lowercase `essential` / `important`. Align entity tiers with legal register.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Grant the saved search `rest` + `edit_saved_search`? actually read-only rest works. Save as `UC-22.2.32`. Use `| rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=local` snippet exactly as in production CIS benchmarks.

```spl
| rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=local
| search title="nis2_*"
```

Throttle alerts to business hours after **14-day** baseline.

Step 3 — Validate
Manually disable a lab correlation search titled `nis2_TEST1`; confirm row returns with `search_disabled=1`. Re-enable and confirm disappearance.

Step 4 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot
If REST returns zero rows, check role lacks ES namespace—error 403 in `splunkd.log`. For SHC, run on deployer-published account with replicated permissions.

Step 5 — Governance
Attach matrix version ID to each export. Sign with `evidenceSigning`. Review quarterly with risk committee; map exceptions to `nis2_exception_register.csv`.


## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_control_matrix.csv
| join entity_tier [
    | inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv
    | stats values(entity_tier) as entity_tiers by legal_entity_id
    | mvexpand entity_tiers
    | rename entity_tiers as entity_tier
]
| where required_for_tier="true"
| join control_id [
    | rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=local
    | search title="nis2_*"
    | eval control_id=replace(title,"nis2_","")
    | stats latest(disabled) as search_disabled by control_id
]
| where search_disabled=1 OR isnull(search_disabled)
| table control_id, entity_tier, search_disabled
```

## Visualization

Matrix heatmap of tier vs control coverage, table of disabled required controls, single value for percentage enabled.
